systems.name: lazysite
systems.tagline: Markdown files served and cached on the fly by a small CGI script.
systems.best_for: Sites that mix content with dynamic behaviour - forms, sign-in, paid pages - and that you would rather edit live, or have an AI manage, than rebuild and redeploy.

systems.name: Hugo
systems.tagline: A very fast static-site generator that compiles Markdown to static files with Go templates.
systems.best_for: Large, purely static sites maintained by developers who want a build pipeline, version-controlled deploys and a big theme ecosystem, and do not need server-side dynamic features.

rows.label: Content format
rows.lazysite: Markdown, rendered and cached on request

rows.label: Build step
rows.lazysite: None - renders on first request, caches the result
rows.hugo: Yes - rebuild the site on every change

rows.label: Dynamic content
rows.lazysite: Server-side: templating, remote data, query parameters, conditionals
rows.hugo: Static only - dynamic needs external JavaScript services

rows.label: Live editing
rows.lazysite: Edit a file over WebDAV or the manager and it is live

rows.label: AI-first management
rows.lazysite: An agent edits live over WebDAV or MCP
rows.hugo: An agent edits source, then a build and deploy must run

rows.label: Templating
rows.lazysite: Template Toolkit, with a separate theme layer

rows.label: Themes
rows.lazysite: A growing library; one file to re-skin

rows.label: Hosting
rows.lazysite: Any CGI host, or its own built-in server

rows.label: Performance
rows.lazysite: Cached static HTML after the first hit

rows.label: Learning curve
rows.hugo: Markdown, plus Go templates and configuration
